principle: The optimal value V*(s_0) is the saddle point of an information-relaxation dual min_m max_pi E_tau[max_t (R_t(tau) - M_t(tau))] where M is the cumulative sum of a martingale-difference penalty m on transitions; learning alternates descent on m and ascent on pi using realized arg-supremum-of-time credit assignment that weights policy gradients only on actions taken on or before the trajectory's penalized-return-maximizing time-step.
primitive_type: S x A x S -> R, with conditional-zero-mean constraint E_{s' ~ p(.|s,a)}[m(s,a,s')] = 0 for all (s,a)
feedback_signal: scalar reward per step plus the realized arg-supremum-time t* of the cumulative penalized-reward process along the trajectory, used to truncate policy-gradient credit assignment
novelty_boundary: REINFORCE/policy-gradient credits actions by cumulative return-to-go G_k; DUAL-IR credits actions by D(tau) * 1[k <= t*(tau)] where D is the realized path-supremum of penalized cumulative reward and t* is its arg-sup-time. These weights differ on any trajectory where R_k - M_k is non-monotone, which is generic when the martingale-difference penalty m_phi is non-trivial. The measurability constraint (conditional zero mean) on m is structural to the information-relaxation duality (Brown-Smith-Sun 2010) and absent in REINFORCE/PG. The ablation m=0 collapses DUAL-IR to a degenerate REINFORCE on monotone-reward envs, so a learning advantage on CartPole would be empirically attributable to the martingale primitive.
empirical_claim: On CartPole-v1 (quick stage, 120s budget), DUAL-IR learns at a rate comparable to or faster than matched-architecture REINFORCE, with at least 30% of episodes during training having arg-supremum-time t* strictly less than terminal time T (a measurable signal that the dual envelope is non-degenerate and credit-truncation actually fires).
falsifier: If the m=0 ablation matches DUAL-IR's CartPole learning curve within seed variation, the martingale-penalty primitive is decorative. If the empirical fraction P(t* < T) stays near zero throughout training, the dual envelope is degenerate and credit-truncation never fires. Either outcome falsifies the probe.
ablation_plan: Replace the martingale-difference penalty m_phi with the constant-zero function (m == 0 identically; remove the penalty network and its optimizer). Keep the rest of the algorithm: still compute D(tau) = max_k R_k, t* = argmax_k R_k, and apply credit-truncated policy gradient weighted by D * 1[k <= t*]. On undiscounted CartPole with strictly-positive per-step reward, R_k is monotone non-decreasing so t* = T identically and the algorithm reduces to REINFORCE with terminal undiscounted return as trajectory weight (no baseline). Compare learning curves and the empirical distribution of t* across seeds.
proof_debt: (1) Existence of a saddle (pi*, m*) for L(pi, m) = E_tau[D(tau; m)] in the joint neural-net parameterization, with L(pi*, m*) = V*(s_0). Brown-Smith-Sun 2010 give weak duality and equality at the value-martingale-increment m*; strong duality for the learning saddle is open. (2) Score-function differentiability through a path supremum: a Danskin-style argument requires almost-sure uniqueness of t*(tau). (3) Convergence of alternating saddle descent: convexity in m is clear, concavity in pi is only at the occupancy-measure level. (4) Bias analysis of the soft conditional-zero-mean constraint enforced by baseline subtraction. (5) Improvement theorem: if the sample dual gap L(pi_n, m_n) - V_{pi_n}(s_0) > 0, the joint update strictly decreases it in expectation, converging to zero.
